qtype: interpreting exponential growth graph and calculation
tex:
When Heidi was born, her grandfather invested some money in an account that paid compound interest.
\newline

The graph shows the exponential growth of this investment.
\newline

(a) Use the graph to find
    (i)  the original amount of money invested,

        

         the number of years it took for the original amount to double,

        

         the value of the investment after 54 years.
\newline

        

(b) This account earned compound interest at a rate of $r\%$ per year.
\newline

    Use your answers to **part (a)(i)** and **part (a)(ii)** to write down an equation in terms of $r$.
\newline

    You do not have to solve your equation.
\newline

content.content: the original amount of money invested,
the number of years it took for the original amount to double,

the value of the investment after 54 years.
\newline

This account earned compound interest at a rate of $r\%$ per year.
\newline

    Use your answers to \textbf{part (a)(i)} and \textbf{part (a)(ii)} to write down an equation in terms of $r$.
\newline

    You do not have to solve your equation.
\newline

tex:
A group of 200 people were asked which city they would like to visit next.
\newline

The table shows the results.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
City & London & Paris & New York & Tokyo \\
\hline
Number of people & 50 & 48 & 56 & 46 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(a) A person from the group is chosen at random.
\newline

    Write down the probability that this person would like to visit either Paris or Tokyo next.
\newline

    

(b) Two people are chosen at random from the group of 200.
\newline

    Find the probability that one person would like to visit London next and the other person would like to visit New York next.
\newline

    Give your answer as a percentage.
\newline

content.content:
A person from the group is chosen at random.
\newline

    Write down the probability that this person would like to visit either Paris or Tokyo next.
\newline

Two people are chosen at random from the group of 200.
\newline

    Find the probability that one person would like to visit London next and the other person would like to visit New York next.
\newline

    Give your answer as a percentage.
\newline

stem.content:
A group of 200 people were asked which city they would like to visit next.
\newline

The table shows the results.

qtype: algebra question, functions, out of scope for Number syllabus
tex:
\[
    f(x) = 7 + 3x \qquad g(x) = x^4 \qquad h(x) = 3^x
\]

(a) $\text{h}(3x) = k^x$

    Find the value of $k$.
\newline

    

(b) Find the value of $x$ when $\text{f}(x) = \text{g}(2)$.
\newline

    

(c) Find $\text{f}^{-1}(x)$.
\newline

stem.content:
\[
    \text{f}(x) = 7 + 3x \qquad \text{g}(x) = x^4 \qquad \text{h}(x) = 3^x
\]
